steps.title: Identify which methodology would artificially inflate the difference
steps.content:
Sweanor (radio-collar tracking, New Mexico): 2.00 cougars per 100 sq km
Choate (helicopter surveying, Utah): 10.24 cougars per 100 sq km
For their 8.24 difference to be artificially large, one method must be systematically undercounting.

steps.title: Determine which method causes underestimation
steps.content: If radio-collar tracking underestimates density, Sweanor's 2.00 is artificially low; the true New Mexico density is higher, making the actual difference smaller than the reported 8.24. Choices A and D address method logistics or prevalence rather than measurement bias; C would cause the gap to be understated, not artificially inflated. That matches choice B — B is correct.
